concerns: Preset bandwidth inconsistency: Plan step 5a returns preset snapshots directly, but PRESET_SNAPSHOTS use raw Mbps (50, 10, 1) while estimateBandwidth normalizes live bandwidth to 0–1. This creates an inconsistent contract on NetworkSnapshot.bandwidth — downstream consumers cannot know whether they're getting raw Mbps or a normalized ratio. Either normalize preset bandwidth values to 0–1 in the preset definitions (separate fix), or have the aggregator normalize the preset bandwidth before returning it.

Plan risk section states 'NetworkSnapshot.bandwidth is consumed as a 0–1 range by snapshotToScene' — but mapSnapshotToScene (src/domain/snapshotToScene.ts) does not read bandwidth at all. The risk justification is inaccurate. The real concern is establishing a consistent contract now so future consumers (likely a geometry builder) can trust the 0–1 range.
Step 3 checks for statusCode === 0 or rttMs === Infinity, but fetchProbe sets rttMs = Infinity on catch. Should use !Number.isFinite(rttMs) instead to also catch NaN edge cases for robustness.

Step 5a should also subscribe to PresetState changes (not just poll on each tick) so that preset activation takes effect immediately rather than waiting up to one full aggregator tick (2 seconds). The existing PresetState.subscribe() API supports this.
